pi_items.source: setup_task.sh line 14 sets DOC_PATH to this exact path. Verified with setup_nuxeo.sh lines 158-162 that Templates workspace is created via REST API at /default-domain/workspaces/Templates. Verified with setup_nuxeo.sh lines 227-230 that Contract-Template document is created within Templates workspace.

task.json metadata field contains these four keywords. Verified against task.json description line 5 which states the comment must be exactly 'Locked for Q4 compliance review - do not modify until review is complete' - all four keywords are present in this required text. Verified in verifier.py line 92 that keywords are used to validate comment content via keyword matching (case-insensitive, line 104).

task.json metadata field specifies 2. Verified in verifier.py line 106 which checks `if len(matches) >= metadata.get('min_keywords_required', 2)` - minimum of 2 matching keywords required for content validation (Criterion 3: 20 pts). The required comment contains all 4 keywords, exceeding the minimum of 2.

setup_nuxeo.sh line 134 copies quarterly_report.pdf to Contract_Template.pdf. The actual file used for this task is quarterly_report.pdf from /examples/nuxeo_platform_env/data/quarterly_report.pdf (size 6.4K, confirmed via file listing). setup_task.sh line 28 looks for Contract_Template.pdf in the mount.

Extracted metadata from quarterly_report.pdf using strings command shows: 'ReportLab Generated PDF document (opensource)' and '/Creator (\(unspecified\))' /CreationDate (D:20260222122538-05'00')'. All PDF files in data directory are synthetic, generated on 2026-02-22. Confirmed with file command showing PDF 1.4 format.

docker-compose.yml line 22 specifies image: nuxeo:10.10. Verified with web search that Nuxeo 10.10 is LTS 2019 (released January 2019). This version is documented in Nuxeo release notes and available on Docker Hub.

export_result.sh line 52 extracts 'lockOwner' from document state. verifier.py line 52 and line 66 use 'lock_owner' from result. Verified with Nuxeo documentation that lockOwner and lockCreated are standard lock properties in document representation.

export_result.sh line 27 uses $NUXEO_URL/api/v1/id/$DOC_UID/@comment endpoint to fetch comments. Verified with Nuxeo REST API documentation (doc.nuxeo.com/nxdoc/comments/) that @comment is the standard adapter for comment operations.

verifier.py lines 99-101 checks for comment text in two possible locations: c.get('text', '') or c['properties'].get('comment:text', ''). These are the standard property paths for comment data in Nuxeo REST API response.

privileged_info_summary: The task 'lock_and_comment_document@1' operates on synthetic PDF data (quarterly_report.pdf generated by ReportLab on 2026-02-22 as Contract Template). It targets Nuxeo Platform version 10.10 (LTS 2019), which has verified lockOwner and lockCreated properties for document locking. The task requires two actions: (1) locking the document at path /default-domain/workspaces/Templates/Contract-Template, and (2) adding a comment containing minimum 2 of the 4 specified keywords: 'compliance', 'review', 'locked', 'do not modify'. The required comment text 'Locked for Q4 compliance review - do not modify until review is complete' contains all four keywords. The verifier checks lock status via REST API (lockOwner property), comment existence via @comment adapter endpoint, and keyword matching via case-insensitive text extraction from comment text or properties.comment:text properties.
